items.title: Marc Bloch: 'Extraordinarily committed to projecting forward' history, the science of men and time
items.description: As historian and WWII resistance fighter Marc Bloch is inducted into the Pantheon, Sharon Gaffney is pleased to welcome Dr. Andrew Smith, Historian of Modern France and Lecturer in Liberal Arts at Queen Mary University of London. Marc Bloch's entry into the Panthéon is described by Andrew Smith not simply as the commemoration of a distinguished historian, but as a profound statement about the contemporary crisis of democracy, truth, and citizenship. Smith argues that Bloch's enduring significance lies in the fusion of scholarship and civic responsibility: he was a historian who viewed the past as a tool for understanding the present, a citizen who defended republican values in times of national collapse, and a resistance fighter who ultimately sacrificed his life for those principles. In Smith's analysis, Bloch's pantheonisation arrives at a moment when France and other democracies confront challenges strikingly reminiscent of those Bloch diagnosed in the twentieth century: distrust of news and information, political polarisation, attacks on minorities, democratic fragility, and the erosion of truth. The ceremony therefore functions simultaneously as a tribute to a beloved intellectual and as a warning from history itself. Bloch emerges not merely as a figure of memory, but as a guide for navigating the democratic uncertainties of the present.

items.description: #France is experiencing its worst heatwave in history, with #Paris hitting above 40 degrees Celsius on June 23. To help its residents cope with the exceptionally high temperatures, the city has opened an area of the #CanalSaintMartin to swimmers. A successful idea, although Parisians say more should be done.

items.title: Ukraine says major Crimea bridge destroyed in latest attack
items.description: Ukraine continues to hit infrastructure on the Crimea to isolate the Russian-annexed peninsula. Meanwhile, Russia attacked Volodymyr Zelenskyy's hometown with a ballistic missile in a deadly strike.

items.title: Host with the most
items.description: Nothing unimportant is more important than football with its victories and defeats metaphors for life and death with nobody actually dying. Leer más

items.title: Colombia’s leftist candidate concedes presidential vote to Trump-backed Abelardo de la Espriella
Scotland: Sturgeon's ex-husband Peter Murrell jailed for 5 years and 3 months
items.description: Peter Murrell pleaded guilty to embezzling funds in his role as chief executive of the Scottish National Party. He held the SNP's purse strings while his then-wife, Nicola Sturgeon, was Scottish First Minister.

items.title: Manuel Adorni asset declaration shows wealth grew 45-fold in 2024
items.description: Cabinet chief’s documentation attempts to explain an astronomical rise in his net worth. His legal strategy and mounting complications in Congress. Leer más

items.title: Clean sweep for Mamdani-backed candidates in New York's Democratic primary
items.description: Brad Lander unseats Dan Goldman in a race that laid bare the city's divisions over the Israel-Gaza war.

items.title: Turkey arrests more than 200 in crackdown before NATO summit
items.description: The Ankara prosecutor's office said 209 people were detained, while 32 others remained at large. Turkey has announced a ban on demonstrations amid preparations for next month's NATO summit in the capital.

items.title: Police release video of Townsville gym shooting and shop fire
items.description: Queensland police have released video of a gym shooting and a suspicious shop fire in Townsville in Feburary.

items.title: Australian police uncover 3 tons of cocaine
items.description: Police found 2.7 metric tons (3 tons) of cocaine on a property on Sydney’s outskirts in Australia’s largest ever seizure of the drug, officials said on Monday.

items.title: UN maritime body starts enacting plan to move sailors out of Gulf
items.description: An evacuation plan to enable hundreds of ships to sail through the Strait of Hormuz is being enacted, according to the UN's International Maritime Organization.

items.title: Massive AI spending and higher interest rates: What's behind the tech sell-off?
items.description: Tech stocks around the world fell on Tuesday, with the flagship Nasdaq index closing 2.2 percent down at the end of the session. In this edition, we look at how markets reacted this Wednesday and some of the reasons why investors sold their shares: massive projected capital expenditure on AI, as well as the prospect of higher interest rates. Also, we examine Spain's pioneering climate leave policy.

items.title: Mamdani-backed candidates defeat pro-Israel lawmakers in primaries
items.description: Candidates backed by Zohran Mamdani have scored major wins in primaries, unseating Democrats backed by pro-Israel groups

items.title: France confirms first Ebola case in doctor returning from DR Congo
items.description: The patient is in isolation, following strict biosafety protocols to prevent risk of spread in France.

items.title: JERA sees LNG prices staying firm as Qatar repairs drag on, Europe rebuilds stocks
Fewer job offers for junior roles due to AI, Swiss study shows
Israeli soldiers ‘celebrate’ inside displaced Lebanese family’s home
items.description: Israeli soldiers ‘celebrate’ inside displaced Lebanese family's home

items.title: More than 3000 Malawian nationals deported in South Africa
items.description: More than 3000 Malawian nationals have been deported in South Africa after thousands protested earlier this year in Johannesburg and other parts of the country against illegal immigration that has fueled tensions between locals and foreign nationals.

items.title: Ukraine’s recovery to be deliberated in Poland amid Kyiv-Warsaw spat
items.description: Amid a brewing row that tests Poland's alliance with Ukraine, President Zelenskyy will skip a key conference.

items.title: Few in U.S. say Iran war was worth it; Trump approval ties lowest of term, Reuters/Ipsos poll finds
items.description: Only 23% of Americans — including just half of Republicans — think the U.S. is now in a stronger position with ‌Iran compared with its position before the war

items.title: Catholic Church urges Congress to reject Milei's property bill
items.description: Church bodies warn that Milei's government's 'Inviolability of Private Property' bill threatens national sovereignty over land, water and natural resources and call on lawmakers to vote it down. Leer más

items.title: Trump administration touts deal with Iran as payday for U.S. farmers; Tehran denies it
items.description: A spokesperson for the Iranian Foreign Ministry, Esmail Baghaei, said any agricultural purchases would be based on "prices and quality," not terms dictated by Washington

items.title: Sri Lanka to appoint military-led dengue monitoring unit amid surge in cases
items.description: Health authorities have reported a total of 46,037 cases across the island, as of June 20

items.title: Heat wave: Alcohol can be a serious danger to your health
items.description: Summer, the FIFA World Cup and a cold beer. Sounds like a dream? Be careful, though; the combination of heat and alcohol can quickly take a very bad turn.

items.title: 'Genocide': UN report says Israel 'deliberately killed Palestinian children'
items.description: PRESS REVIEW – Wednesday, June 24: Papers discuss the newest UN report that says Israel targeted and killed Palestinian children after the truce. Next: more than 40 people have drowned amid the heatwave in France. Also: France's national team coach Didier Deschamps will skip an important game following the death of his mother. Finally, we take a look at the world's loudest man.

items.title: Hungary passes anti-graft measures, eyeing EU funds
items.description: Pro-EU conservative Magyar ousted Orban from office after 16 years in power in an April election on a promise of "regime change". The anti-graft legislation easily passed parliament with 142 votes for, 39 against and three abstentions

items.title: America’s Mamdani moment
items.description: Zohran Mamdani’s victory in New York, a city with a budget of over $100 billion and a population larger than that of 40 U.S. States, demonstrates that the political movement has transcended niche activism to achieve mainstream electoral viability

items.title: Messi eyes World Cup record and birthday win against Austria
items.description: Albiceleste captain celebrates his birthday on Wednesday and he will hope to hold the World Cup scoring record outright by then. Leer más

items.title: Israel gets thrown under the bus
items.description: Unlike the US president and his business cronies, the Israelis have no illusions about the fanatical Iranian regime. Leer más

items.title: Gina Rinehart names veterans' accommodation after Ben Roberts-Smith
items.description: Australia's richest person has opened a facility for homeless veterans in a beachside Perth suburb.

items.title: In a first, U.S. Senate approves War Powers resolution in rebuke to Trump over Iran conflict
items.description: It was the 10th time the Senate has tried to stop the war, and the outcome, on a vote of 50-48, was a stunning turnaround from past efforts

items.title: Biggest city in Crimea without power after Ukraine strikes
items.description: Sevastopol's Moscow-installed governor warns there will be no electricity in some areas until the evening.

items.title: Japanese sake brewery to make foray into Indian market
items.description: Takahashi Shouten, based in the city of Yame in Fukuoka Prefecture, launched a subsidiary in India in February.

items.title: Milei to stand by his man as calls for Adorni’s sacking grow
items.description: Former ally Ramiro Marra becomes latest voice to call for official’s resignation; Mounting problems for top official, who admitted he failed to declare cash savings and is struggling to explain his expenditure. Leer más

items.title: Watch: HL: Lucknow building fire: FIR cites illegal commercial use | Above the Fold | 23.06.2026
items.description: In this episode, we look at the devastating Lucknow building fire and the broader questions it raises about fire safety in India’s cities. We also examine the Centre’s latest FCRA amendments, Meta’s elevation of CRED founder Kunal Shah to lead WhatsApp, and more.

items.title: Guinea bans export of raw gold in bid to boost revenues
items.description: Guinea outlaws the export of raw gold in a bid to increase domestic processing of the valuable natural resource.

items.title: France on red alert for forest fires amid extreme heat
items.description: France is in the midst of a record-breaking heatwave, with yesterday being the hottest day on record in the country. The extreme heat is also creating drought and heightening the risk of forest fires, with multiple departments, namely in the southwest, on high alert. Forest fires have already broken out, with one now under control and firefighters still working to try and control another blaze.
